_doc: Conformance fixture for spec §10.4 — Phase 1 public API for extra redaction suffixes. Cross-binding parity: dagstack-logger (Python), @dagstack/logger (TypeScript), go.dagstack.dev/logger (Go) MUST produce identical wire output for each case.
_validation_error_match_doc: validation_failures[].expected_error is matched as a CASE-INSENSITIVE SUBSTRING against the binding's raised error message. Bindings are free to wrap, prefix, or localise the error provided that the substring appears verbatim. A future spec revision may introduce stable error codes via a separate `_meta/error_reasons.yaml` (config-spec pattern); until then substring matching is the cross-binding contract.

cases.name: additive_default
cases.description: extra_suffixes is unioned with the base set; non-matching keys pass through.

cases.name: replace_defaults_narrows_set
cases.description: replace_defaults=true swaps the base set for the supplied list — base patterns no longer mask.

cases.name: additive_recurses_into_nested_dict
cases.description: Per §10.2 redaction is recursive; an extra suffix matches at any depth.

cases.name: additive_recurses_into_list_of_maps
cases.description: Per §10.2 list-of-map traversal — an extra suffix matches keys inside list elements.

cases.name: case_insensitive_match
cases.description: Suffix match is case-insensitive on the key, per §10.1.

cases.name: replace_defaults_with_empty_extra_disables_redaction
cases.description: replace_defaults=true with an empty extra_suffixes list turns off all suffix-based redaction. Allowed but drastic; downstream auditing should warn on this configuration.
